ВҠВҮВҺВҮВҗВүВ–ВҠВ•В‘ВҲВ–ВҠВҮВ•ВӢВҶВҮВ•ЬіЬҙЗЎЬҙЬІВғВҗВҶЬІЬіВӢВҗВ–В”ВӢВғВҗВүВҺВҮЬІЬіЬҙВғВ”ВҮЬҪЗЎЬҪ аө… ЭҖВғВҗВҶЬҪ аө… 2ЭҖ
       В”ВҮВ•В’ВҮ В–ВӢВҳВҮВҺВӣЗЎВҷВҠВҮВ”ВҮЬҪВғВҗВҶЭҖВғВ”ВҮВ’В‘В•ВӢВ–ВӢВҳВҮВғВҗВҶВ•В— ВҠВ–ВҠВғВ–3ЭҖ аөҗ 2ЬҪЗӨ
       
       ВҠВғВ–ВӢВ•В–ВҠВҮВҲВ—ВҺВҺВ”ВғВҗВүВҮЗЎВӢВҗВҶВҮВүВ”ВҮВҮВ•ЗЎВ‘ВҲВ’В‘В•В•ВӢВ„ВҺВҮВҳВғВҺВ—ВҮВ•ВҲВ‘В”ВғВҗВүВҺВҮЬІЬҙЬіЗ«

solution.rawText:
It would be really helpful to sketch this triangle so that we donвҖҷt get confused! We will write Оё
for the angle вҲ P RQ.

                                                      R

                                                      Оё       a
                                      a+d
                                                                    Q

                                                 a + 2d
                                 P


It is clear that we want to use the cosine rule to relate the three sides and the angle Оё. Using
вҖҳc2 = a2 + b2 вҲ’ 2ab cos CвҖҷ, we have

                             (a + 2d)2 = a2 + (a + d)2 вҲ’ 2a(a + d) cos Оё

which we can expand and simplify to give

                          a2 + 4ad + 4d2 = 2a2 + 2ad + d2 вҲ’ 2a(a + d) cos Оё

Rearranging gives
                                                 a2 вҲ’ 2ad вҲ’ 3d2
                                       cos Оё =
                                                    2a(a + d)
We can simplify this by noticing that the numerator factorises:

                                            (a + d)(a вҲ’ 3d)   a вҲ’ 3d
                                  cos Оё =                   =
                                               2a(a + d)        2a

Since 3d > 2a, a вҲ’ 3d < вҲ’a, and so cos Оё < вҲ’a     1                     в—Ұ
                                           2a = вҲ’ 2 . This gives Оё > 120 , and so the answer
must be option E.
We can go further and show that Оё < 180в—Ұ . Since P QR is a triangle, the length P Q must be less
than the sum of P R and RQ, that is a + 2d < a + d + a, so d < a. This gives cos Оё > вҲ’2a
                                                                                      2a = вҲ’1
so Оё < 180в—Ұ .

An alternative approach is to just try values for d. We are given that d > 23 a, so we could try
d = 23 a. This gives side lengths of a, 53 a, 73 a, which we can take to be 3, 5, 7 if we choose a = 3.
Plugging these lengths into the cosine rule gives Оё = 120в—Ұ , so this is one limit.
We are allowed larger values of d. If we take d too large, then we will no longer have a triangle,
as P Q > P R + RQ. So as d increases, the angle Оё must increase too. Therefore the answer must
be E: 120в—Ұ < Оё < 180в—Ұ .
